what_is_open: Simplify the exact CAD/Thom branch output into a compact human-readable region-by-region symbolic atlas for every globally active three-point support cell, including interpretable inequalities and closed-form formulas for each cell.
obstruction: The constructive algorithm proves existence, computability, and exact certification by solving the finite semialgebraic branch programs, but CAD cell decompositions and Thom encodings need not collapse to short symbolic inequalities. No frozen primitive, established theorem, or available closed-form branch calculation supplies a compact printed atlas for the full three-point region of the quartic coefficient space.
attempted: The proof uses the available complete route: support-at-most-three reduction, the explicit fixed-second-moment profile H(s), exact singleton/two-point endpoint reduction, and semialgebraic CAD/Thom optimization and certification. These close the design-construction theorem but stop short of symbolic simplification of every three-point cell.
partial_result: For every theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4) with 0<=pbar<=1, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm returns a globally optimal saturation law supported on at most three points and certifies it. The profile H(s) is explicit by lem:explicit-profile-value, and the singleton/two-point cells are closed form by prop:explicit-two-point-cell.
